* 100)\\n\\n    warmup_steps = min(max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps)), max(1, total_steps // 2))\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step_idx: int) -> float:\\n        if step_idx < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(step_idx + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (step_idx - warmup_steps) / float(max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps))\\n        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Within a fixed 256-step budget, the baseline effectively uses a constant small LR and under-utilizes early optimization.
